definition: Percentage of customers lost during a given period.
business_question: How many customers are we losing over time?
formula_logic: Lost Clients ÷  AVERAGE (Beginning client, Ending client)

definition: Percentage of recurring revenue lost from churned customers.
business_question: How much recurring revenue are we losing due to churn?

definition: Percentage decrease in recurring revenue from downgrades or partial churn.
business_question: Are customers reducing their subscriptions?

definition: Percentage increase in recurring revenue from existing customers.
business_question: Are existing customers increasing their spend with us?

definition: Average cost to acquire one new customer.
business_question: How efficient is our customer acquisition?

definition: Estimated total revenue generated by a customer over their lifetime.
business_question: How much revenue does an average customer generate long-term?
formula_logic: ((Average MRR per New Client) *(GM%))  ÷  (AVERAGE MRR CHURN %)

definition: Average recurring revenue lost when a customer churns.
business_question: Are we losing high-value customers?
formula_logic: Σ  Lost Clients MRR  ÷  Σ Count of Lost Clients

definition: Average recurring revenue generated by newly acquired customers.
business_question: Are we acquiring high-value customers?
formula_logic: Σ  New Clients MRR  ÷  Σ Count of New Clients

definition: The average recurring revenue generated per active customer.
business_question: How valuable is an average customer to our business today?
formula_logic: Ending Balance Organic MRR ÷  Count of Ending Clients

definition: Time required to recover customer acquisition costs.
business_question: How long does it take to break even on customer acquisition?
formula_logic: (Average CAC ÷ Average MRR per New Client) x Gross Profit

definition: Estimated average duration a customer stays subscribed.
business_question: How long do customers typically stay with us?

definition: Compares customer lifetime value to acquisition cost.
business_question: Are customers worth more than what it costs to acquire them?

definition: The net change in monthly recurring revenue during a period.
business_question: How valuable is an average customer to our business today?
formula_logic: Σ (New, Upsell, Expansion, Contractions, Lost clients and Implem churn MRR)

definition: Measures how much recurring revenue is retained from existing customers over time, including expansions and contractions.
business_question: Are we growing revenue from our existing customers, even after churn and downgrades?
formula_logic:
Monthly = Σ (Starting MRR, Upsell, Expansion, Contraction, Lost Clients MRR)  ÷ 
Starting MRR

12 months rolling = Σ (Beginning MRR, Net MRR Change) ÷  Beginning MRR

definition: Indicates how efficiently the company is growing recurring revenue relative to revenue losses.
business_question: Is our revenue growth strong enough to offset churn and contraction?
formula_logic:
Σ (Starting MRR, Upsell, Expansion MRR  ÷ 
Σ (Contraction, Lost Clients, Implem Churn)

definition: Evaluates how effectively sales and marketing spend is converted into new recurring revenue.
business_question: Are our sales and marketing investments generating efficient revenue growth?
formula_logic:
Σ (Starting MRR, Upsell, Expansion MRR) ÷  
Σ (Contraction, Lost Clients, Implem Churn)

definition: : Total cost incurred to acquire new customers.
business_question: How much are we spending to acquire customers?

definition: % of leads marked Disqualified
business_question: Are we targeting the wrong audience?
filters_inclusions:
Count as Qualified = Yes
Deal Stage = All

definition: Percentage of total leads that convert into Closed-Won deals.
business_question: How effective is the full funnel from lead to revenue?
filters_inclusions: Leads created in selected period

definition: Leads qualified by Marketing using BANT with Probability to Buy ≥ 40
business_question: How many marketing leads are sales-ready?
filters_inclusions:
Count as Qualified = Yes
Department Source = MKT Inbound

definition: Leads qualified by LDU Outbound using BANT with Probability to Buy ≥ 40
business_question: How many outbound leads are qualified?
filters_inclusions:
Count as Qualified = Yes
Department Source = LDU Outbound

definition: Leads qualified by Marketing/ LDU/ Sales
business_question: How many leads are sales-ready?
filters_inclusions: Count as Qualified = Yes

definition: % of Qualified Leads that become SQL
business_question: How effective is lead handoff to Sales?
filters_inclusions: Same create date for QL & SQL

definition: Deals disqualified by Sales during demo stage
business_question: Are Sales rejecting poor-quality SQLs?
filters_inclusions:
Count as Qualified = Yes
Deal Stage = Disqualified

definition: Also known as SQL are leads that the Sales team will work on to convert them into clients.
business_question: How many leads did Sales accept to work on?
filters_inclusions:
Count as Qualified = Yes
Deal Stage:  Initial Discovery Done, Solutioning, Commercial Negotiation, Committed Accounts, Contract Signed, Project Handover and Closed lost

definition: Leads qualified by Marketing/ CSM/ Sales
business_question: How many leads are sales-ready?
filters_inclusions: Pipeline = PH Upsell Pipeline

definition: Percentage of Sales Qualified Leads (SQL/ACTIVE) that converted into Closed-Won deals. SQL count is based on customers that are currently active within the selected month.
business_question: Of the active qualified pipeline this month, how many converted into paying customers?
filters_inclusions:
SQL/ACTIVE status = Active in selected period
WON = Deal Stage is Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn

definition: Average marketing cost per lead generated
business_question: How much do we spend per lead?

definition: Percentage of companies in an industry that purchased a specific product.
business_question: Which products resonate most within each industry vertical?
formula_logic: Companies in Industry with Product ÷ Total Companies in Industry
filters_inclusions:
Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn
Product Status = Availed, MRR > 0

definition: Percentage of companies that purchased Product B among companies that purchased Product A.
business_question: If a customer buys Product A, how likely are they to also buy Product B?
formula_logic: Companies with Product A & B ÷ Companies with Product A
filters_inclusions:
Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn
Product Status = Availed, MRR > 0

definition: Percentage of companies in a segment that purchased a specific product.
business_question: Which products are most adopted within ENT, SME, or Micro segments?
formula_logic: Companies in Segment with Product ÷ Total Companies in Segment
filters_inclusions:
Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn
Product Status = Availed, MRR > 0

definition: Expected yearly recurring revenue from signed clients
business_question: How much recurring revenue are we generating annually?
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn

definition: ReadyCash/ ReadyWage monthly recurring revenue from signed proposal. Recognized only if contract term ≥ 12 months.
business_question: How much recurring revenue are we generating monthly for EF?
formula_logic: Sum of MRR where Term ≥ 12 months
filters_inclusions:
Closed Won; Term ≥ 12 months
Product Name = ReadyCash or ReadyWage

definition: Monthly recurring revenue from signed proposal. Recognized only if contract term ≥ 12 months.
business_question: How much recurring revenue are we generating monthly?
formula_logic: Sum of MRR where Term ≥ 12 months
filters_inclusions: Closed Won; Term ≥ 12 months

definition: One-Time Payment represents revenue from line items billed as “One Time Payment” and non-OTP products with contract terms less than 12 months, treated as one-time equivalent revenue.
business_question: How much one-time or short-term revenue was generated from eligible line items?
filters_inclusions:
Closed Won; Term < 12 months &
Billing Freq = "one time payment"

definition: Full contract value inclusive of recurring & one-time
business_question: What is total signed revenue?
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn

definition: Average MRR of won deals
business_question: What is the average revenue per won deal?
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn

definition: The average number of days it takes to close a deal, measured from deal creation to deal close date.
business_question: How long does it take, on average, to close a deal?
formula_logic: Average of (Close Date − Create Date) for won deals
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover,  Sales Churn and Closed lost

definition: Determines the health of the sales pipeline relative to the sales target. The industry standard for the coverage ratio is at 5X the target for the full year and 3x for the quarter.
business_question: Do we have enough pipeline to hit target?
filters_inclusions: Future Close Date; Active stages

definition: A deal pursued by sales but did not successfully close to becoming a client.
business_question: Why are we losing deals?
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage: Closed lost

definition: Total MRR of all active pipeline deals
business_question: How large is our potential recurring pipeline?
formula_logic: Sum of MRR for active stages
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage:  Initial Discovery Done, Solutioning, Commercial Negotiation, Committed Accounts

definition: Percentage of assigned quota achieved by a rep or team.
business_question: Are reps hitting their revenue targets?
filters_inclusions: Closed-Won revenue

definition: % of Contract Signed deals that did not proceed to Implementation within 60 days.
business_question: How much revenue is lost due to stalled onboarding?
formula_logic: Churned Deals ÷ Total Contract Signed Deals
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn

definition: % of existing customers that purchased additional products.
business_question: How effective are we at expanding revenue within existing accounts?
formula_logic: Customers with Upsell ÷ Total Active Customers
filters_inclusions:
Client Stage: Customer Success 
Pipeline: Upsell
Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn

definition: % of won deals against all closed deals
business_question: How effective is Sales at closing deals?
filters_inclusions:
Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover,  Sales Churn and Closed lost
Close Date

definition: Expected pipeline revenue based on deal probability
business_question: What revenue can we realistically expect to close?
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage:  Initial Discovery Done, Solutioning, Commercial Negotiation, Committed Accounts

definition: Deals completed with a signed proposal
business_question: How many deals were successfully closed?
filters_inclusions: Deal Stage: Contract Signed, Project Handover and Sales Churn
